requirement: the format specification is well-structured and readable

findings.observation: Document ordering is logical and concept-before-detail: Conformance, Terminology, Document Structure, Frontmatter Schema, Body Semantics, Evaluation Semantics, Report Semantics, Extensions, Appendices.
Two appendices provide minimal and realistic examples, clearly marked non-normative; prose throughout is plain and declarative.

Gap: requiredness is conveyed via YAML code-comment annotations rather than true field tables with type/required/cardinality/default columns, so allowed-values and defaults are not uniformly tabulated.
Gap: heading levels are inconsistent (Model/Target/Factor/Requirement at H4 directly under an H2, skipping an intervening level).

rationale: Dependency-respecting structure, scannable schema blocks, plain prose, and realistic examples satisfy the requirement; a few structural nits (annotation-vs-table, heading hierarchy) keep it from outstanding.
